title: Lysogeny broth (LB) Agar Plate Preparation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7h5hf","text":"Luria broth (LB) is a nutrient-rich media commonly used to culture bacteria in the lab. The addition of agar to LB results in the formation of a gel that bacteria can grow on, as they are unable to digest the agar but can gather nutrition from the LB within. The addition of an antibiotic to this gel allows for the selection of only those bacteria with resistance to that antibiotic - usually conferred by a plasmid carrying the antibiotic resistance gene. The following protocol will allow you to make your own LB/agar plates with your antibiotic of interest.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4cs2h","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4fvgc","text":"https://www.addgene.org/protocols/pouring-lb-agar-plates/","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":57}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"E0C8B4601DAD11F194F00A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.addgene.org/protocols/pouring-lb-agar-plates"}}}}
steps: Collect a 1 L bottle with top.
Ensure the bottle is clean by rinsing with DI H2O

To prepare 0.5 L, dissolve 5 g tryptone, 2.5 g yeast extract, 5 g NaCl , and 7.5 g of agar in 0.250 L of deionized water and fill to 0.5 L Have a little water in the bottle before you add the dry powder.
Place autoclave tape on the lid of the bottle and autoclave at 121°C for 20-30 minutes Make sure the lid is loosened to avoid pressure build up.
Place empty sterile plates around a flame. Allow the LB media to cool to < 50 C

A B C 1 Antibiotic Recommended Stock Concentration Recommended Working Concentration 2 Ampicillin 100 mg/mL 100 µg/mL 3 Bleocin 5 mg/mL 5 µg/mL 4 Carbenicillin* 100 mg/mL 100 µg/mL 5 Chloramphenicol 25 mg/mL (dissolve in EtOH) 25 µg/mL 6 Coumermycin 25 mg/mL (dissolve in DMSO) 25 µg/mL 7 Gentamycin 10 mg/mL 10 µg/mL 8 Kanamycin 50 mg/mL 50 µg/mL 9 Spectinomycin 50 mg/mL 50 µg/mL 10 Tetracycline 10 mg/mL 10 µg/mL Unless otherwise indicated, the antibiotic powder can be dissolved in dH 2 O. *Carbenicillin can be used in place of ampicillin. Carbenicillin is more stable, so it is potentially more effective at selecting only bacteria containing the plasmids of interest (for example, fewer satellite colonies will grow). However, it is also more expensive. Cite: https://www.addgene.org/protocols/pouring-lb-agar-plates/

Allow the LB media to cool to <50 C then add the appropriate antibiotic (see next section) The media is sterile at this point. DO NOT insert a thermometer. When bubbles remain when it swirls it is around the right temperature.
Pour the media into the plates and allow it to solidify before closing the lid completely
Label the plates with the date it was poured and the antibiotic(s) it contains
Store at 4 C until use. If the plate noticeably changes color, appears dehydrated, or has growth immediately dispose in the biohazard waste.
creator: Tamra Mbeuh Lahom Lot
